title: Myristica malayana
taxonomy: Myristica malayana is a species of plant that belongs to the family Myristicaceae. This family consists of a group of evergreen trees and shrubs commonly known as the nutmeg family. The Myristicaceae family is part of the order Magnoliales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Magnoliales is further classified under the class Magnoliopsida, which includes a wide range of flowering plant species. The class Magnoliopsida is part of the kingdom Plantae, which encompasses all plant species.
uses: Myristica malayana is traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine for its potential health benefits. Its extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to possess wound-healing properties and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its leaves are also used in traditional medicine to treat skin conditions and wounds. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential applications of Myristica malayana.

Its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in managing various health conditions. The plant is also thought to have anti-cancer properties, inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis. side_effects: Myristica malayana is a plant species native to Borneo, and its essential oils have been used in traditional medicine, but the available information on its side effects is limited. However, as with many essential oils, it may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, or interact with certain medications in some individuals. Moreover, ingesting large quantities of Myristica malayana essential oil can be toxic, leading to symptoms like nausea, vomiting, or even more severe health issues.
